rawContent: {"stage":"build","insights":[{"kind":"lesson","text":"withAuth guarantees a non-null user; redundant if (!user) guards after withAuth can be removed to simplify code.","scope":"auth"},{"kind":"convention","text":"CSRF token endpoints intentionally skip withAuth — they validate session via x-session-id header, not JWT. This is an explicit allowlist exception, not an oversight.","scope":"auth"},{"kind":"lesson","text":"withAuth with no roles option means 'any authenticated user' — this is correct for user-specific resources where identity alone suffices.","scope":"auth"}]}
